name: Giant Nobel
description: A large, smooth-leaf spinach producing very broad, flat leaves with a mild flavor. Preferred for cooking over savoyed types because the smooth leaves are easier to clean and wilt more evenly.
history: A Dutch heirloom dating to the early 20th century, bred for large leaf production in European market gardens. The 'Nobel' designation indicated top quality in Dutch horticultural nomenclature of the era.
seasonal_quirks: Bolts faster than Bloomsdale in heat — best for early spring and fall. Very cold-hardy. Overwinters in mild climates with minimal protection.
harvest_cues: Leaves are best at 4–6 inches for fresh salads. Allow to grow larger for cooking — leaves stay tender even at 8–10 inches.
variety_notes: The large flat leaves are ideal for blanching whole for stuffed pasta applications. Smooth surface makes washing much easier than savoyed types.
preservation: Blanch and freeze. Spinach reduces to a fraction of fresh volume — grow plenty.
